attribution_note: Credit BMKG as the data source in the app and any published materials.

feeds.description: List of recent significant earthquakes (BMKG definition, typically M 5.0+)

response_shape.notes: Does NOT include Dirasakan (felt reports) or Shakemap
Includes Dirasakan (felt/MMI reports) but NOT Shakemap

danger_classification.overview: These rules determine when a BMKG earthquake event should trigger the emergency alarm + TTS briefing in the Prometheus app. Any event matching at least one CRITICAL or HIGH rule is classified as dangerous.

data_source_notes.autogempa: Use this feed for real-time monitoring (poll every 60s). It provides the most complete data including Dirasakan and Shakemap.
data_source_notes.gempaterkini: Use for historical context. No Dirasakan field available.
data_source_notes.gempadirasakan: Use for felt-intensity cross-check. All events here have Dirasakan data.

rules.condition: Potensi field contains any of ['tsunami', 'berpotensi tsunami', 'berpotensi'] AND does NOT start with or contain 'tidak'
rules.rationale: Any confirmed tsunami potential from BMKG is immediately dangerous regardless of magnitude
example_non_matches: Gempa ini dirasakan untuk diteruskan pada masyarakat

rules.rationale: Earthquakes M 6.0+ can cause damage even at moderate depth

rules.condition: Magnitude (parsed as float) >= 5.0 AND depth_km (parsed as integer) < 70
rules.rationale: M 5.0+ at shallow depth (< 70 km) is strongly felt and can cause damage near the epicenter

rules.condition: Dirasakan field contains any MMI value >= V (Roman numeral), i.e. matches regex pattern of Roman numeral >= V or Arabic numeral >= 5 combined with recognized location names
rules.rationale: MMI V+ indicates moderate to strong shaking that can cause damage to vulnerable structures

mmi_reference.VI: Strong, light damage in weak buildings
mmi_reference.VII: Very strong, damage in well-built buildings

rules.rationale: M 5.0+ at depth >= 70 km is felt but rarely causes damage. Informational only — does NOT trigger alarm.

classification_logic.trigger_alarm: ANY rule with severity CRITICAL or HIGH matches
classification_logic.log_only: Only LOW or INFO rules match — no alarm, but may be logged for user reference
classification_logic.update_cadence: Poll autogempa feed every 60 seconds while app is in foreground. On each new event different from previous (by DateTime), re-evaluate.

implementation_notes: Magnitude arrives as a string like '2.9' or '6.0'. Parse as Float before comparison.
Kedalaman arrives as a string like '10 km'. Parse integer depth by extracting digits before ' km'.
Dirasakan uses Roman numerals (I through XII) followed by location names, e.g. 'III Kolaka Timur, II-III Kolaka'. Extract the max Roman numeral value.
The autogempa feed has the most complete data (includes Dirasakan and Shakemap). Use it as the primary feed.
When comparing new vs previous events, use the DateTime field (ISO 8601) as the unique identifier.
Coordinates arrive as '-4.08,121.79' format (latitude,longitude). Both use negative for South/West.
Bujur (longitude) suffix: 'BT' = East, 'BB' = West (though Indonesia is entirely East).

Define user GPS proximity rule (optional) — only alert if epicenter is within X km of user
